# Flowmoat

> Flowmoat is an enterprise AI engineering company that builds AI-powered software,
> modernizes legacy systems, and automates business workflows for organizations of
> 50–1,000 employees across the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, Australia,
> and the United Arab Emirates.

Flowmoat combines product thinking with enterprise engineering. Engagements start with the
business outcome, then work backward to the right architecture. Flowmoat also builds and
operates its own SaaS products, which means its team has firsthand experience turning complex
business domains into production software rather than delivering isolated features.

## Services

- [AI Engineering](https://flowmoat.com/#services): Intelligent systems, AI agents, and copilots that help teams find answers, make decisions, and execute complex work with human oversight.
- [AI Modernization](https://flowmoat.com/#services): Add practical AI to existing CRMs, ERPs, portals, SaaS products, mobile apps, and legacy systems without a disruptive rebuild.
- [Enterprise Software Development](https://flowmoat.com/#services): Custom SaaS, internal platforms, business portals, dashboards, back-office and administration systems.
- [Workflow Automation](https://flowmoat.com/#services): Business process automation, API integration, and custom workflow engineering that connects people, systems, and data.

## Products

- [RegRely](https://regrely.com): AI-powered compliance and privacy management platform for compliance programs, privacy operations, vendor risk, and governance.
- [GreenOH](https://greenoh.pk): Pakistan's first solar aggregator platform, where homes and businesses compare verified solar installers through one trusted marketplace.

## Who Flowmoat works with

Founders, CEOs, CTOs, COOs, VP Engineering, VP Operations, and IT Directors at growing companies
with meaningful software or workflow complexity. Typical engagements range from $10,000 to $100,000+.

## How engagements work

1. **Discover** — Map the objective, current workflow, users, systems, data, constraints, and success criteria.
2. **Architect** — Define the product experience, technical approach, integration boundaries, controls, and phased delivery plan.
3. **Build** — Deliver working software in focused iterations, with regular demonstrations and transparent decisions.
4. **Scale** — Support rollout, measure performance, strengthen the system, and evolve it as the business grows.

## Contact

- [Book a strategy call](https://flowmoat.com/#contact)
